content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob have got an integer NNN. They decide to play a game. Alice and Bob make alternating moves: Alice makes the first move, Bob makes the second move, Alice makes the third one, and so on. The game continues until NNN becomes 000. The player who is unable to make a move loses.

During each turn a player can make one of the following moves:



Choose an integer XXX such that XXX can be expressed as 2Y2^Y2Y, Y≥1Y \geq 1Y≥1. The chosen XXX must also be a factor of NNN. After choosing an integer XXX which satisfies the mentioned criteria, the player will divide NNN by XXX.




If Move 111 is not possible , the player will subtract 111 from NNN.



Predict the winner of the game if the game is played optimally by both the players.


Input Format


The first line of input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN.





Output Format
For each test case, print "Alice" if Alice wins the game else print "Bob". Print the output without quotes.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Alice can't perform the first move, hence she subtracts 1 from NNN and NNN becomes 000. Bob can't make a move now.
Test case 111: Alice first divides NNN by 222 , after which NNN becomes 111. Bob, in the next move decrements NNN by 111 after which NNN becomes 000 and Alice loses.
